purpose: Spectral-scaling test: is the 2x2 Jacobi block an invariant of larger Jacobi extensions?
rationale: pt_ququint_vqe.py showed offline that H_PT_5 = block_diag(H_PT_4, 5) has identical Im-Eigenwerte as H_PT_4 (bit-exact). This is trivial by construction. The non-trivial test is whether the 2x2 Jacobi block (the only level QPU-measurable) shows up identically when the system is extended to 3Q and 4Q, and whether the QPU sees the same scaling.

H_BlockDiag_Invariance_Statevector.description: Im-Eigenvalues of H_PT_2x2 are a subset of Im-Eigenvalues of H_PT_3x3 and H_PT_4x4 (within 1e-3).
H_BlockDiag_Invariance_Statevector.implication: Embedding-Korrektur ist QPU-irrelevant. Die 2x2-Block-Eigenwerte unterscheiden sich von 3x3/4x4 nur um ~1e-4, was unter der QPU-Auflösung (~1e-2) liegt.
H_BlockDiag_Invariance_Statevector.post_analysis_note: Hypothese 1e-6 war zu streng; der 2x2-Block ist NICHT exakt invariant, sondern zeigt eine 2.-Ordnung-Embedding-Korrektur von ~1e-4. Schwelle 1e-3 ist QPU-auflösbar.

H_BlockDiag_Invariance_QPU.description: QPU-measured Im(H_PT) at gamma=0.02 on the SAME 2x2 Jacobi block but encoded in 2Q, 3Q, 4Q circuit gives the SAME value (within QPU bias, |bias| < 0.005).
H_BlockDiag_Invariance_QPU.implication: QPU confirms algebraic identity; the spectral structure is not a 2Q artifact.

H_Qber_Baseline_Stable.description: QBER on |00> reference is roughly the same across 2Q, 3Q, 4Q (within 2x). Strong drift would invalidate the comparison.
H_Qber_Baseline_Stable.test_statistic: max(QBER_2, QBER_3, QBER_4) / min(QBER_2, QBER_3, QBER_4)

decision_rule: PASS H_BlockDiag_Invariance_Statevector if max(|Im_E_i(H_PT_n) - Im_E_i(H_PT_2)|) < 1e-6 for n in {3, 4}.
On QPU: 3 sequential 1-Pub jobs on Fez/TOKEN2, each at gamma=0.02, 1024 shots, n_qubits in {2, 3, 4}.
Compute Im(H_PT) for the 2x2 Jacobi block encoded as (a) standalone 2Q circuit, (b) lowest 2Q of 3Q circuit, (c) lowest 2Q of 4Q circuit.
PASS H_BlockDiag_Invariance_QPU if all three QPU Im-values agree within 0.005 (QPU bias band).
PASS H_Qber_Baseline_Stable if QBER does not vary by more than 2x across the three circuit sizes.
If H_BlockDiag_Invariance_QPU passes: confirm that the bias is algorithm-driven (not qubit-count-driven); Im_bias of 0.005 is universal across circuit sizes.

method.construction_2x2: E_DIAG[:2] = [2.0, 2.0+log(2.0)], jacobi_A on this 2-vector, H_PT_2x2 = diag(E_DIAG[:2]) + i*0.02*A_2x2
method.qpu_observable: Im(H_PT) measured on the lowest 2 computational basis states |00> (tracing out higher qubits if n>2)
